entry.title: Translation of User Defined Fields in B1
entry.content:
I have a column in the payment terms (OCTG) called "U_ShowPayment".

I set up values that I can find with 

select * from "UFD1" where "TableID" = 'OCTG';

They have translations that I can merry with

select * from
  "OMLT" T1
  LEFT JOIN "MLT1" T2 ON T2."TranEntry" = T1."TranEntry"
  WHERE T1."TableName" = 'UFD1' AND T1."FieldAlias" = 'Descr'
;

But how can I get from my entry in OCTG to the translation in MLT1? There must be some tables in between. Is there some graphical Documentation showing how those translation tables relate?

entry.title: S/4 HANA BAPI_PO_CREATE1 COND ERROR
entry.content:
아래는 작성해주신 내용을 영문으로 자연스럽게 정리한 번역본입니다.

We are facing an issue after converting from ECC to S/4HANA.

Purchase Orders are created through an interface using BAPI_PO_CREATE1.

The error occurs in the following scenario:

  • Account assignment category is related to assets (A, Y, Z).

  • There is no material number.

  • The purchase order item is created as a text item (free-text).

In ECC, under the same situation, PBXX was automatically determined via BAPI_PO_CREATE1 and the PO was created without any issue.

However, in S/4HANA, the system is unable to determine the unit price and raises error message 06 215 (Enter net price).

To resolve this, we tried to forcibly populate PBXX in the COND and CONDX structures within the BAPI logic.
Despite this, the same error still occurs.

 

I believe the cause is that the exit flag is broken within BAPI_PO_CRETE1, and the source within PERFORM GET_CURRENT_DATA is not being used. I'm only focusing on the single cause.

We would like to understand the root cause and identify a solution for this issue.

entry.title: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error on OData $metadata in SAP Build Work Zone (BTP ABAP Trial)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am deploying a SAPUI5 application to SAP Build Work Zone using the Managed AppRouter, connected to the BTP ABAP Trial environment.

The app works perfectly locally in BAS, but in Work Zone, it fails to load data. The browser console shows an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error when fetching the OData $metadata and Fiori annotations.

Log-dbg.js:497 2026-02-23 14:21:14.553100 [ODataMetadata] initial loading of metadata failed
Log-dbg.js:497 2026-02-23 14:21:14.553500 Error: HTTP request failed

My Configuration:

  • Destination has HTML5.DynamicDestination = true and WebIDEEnabled = true.

  • Destination has HTML5.ForwardAuthToken = true.

  • My xs-app.json route uses "authenticationType": "xsuaa".

  • I have already cleared the Work Zone site cache.

I have attached my manifest.json, routing file, and a screenshot of my Destination configuration for reference. Any help is greatly appreciated!

manifest.json

{
  "_version": "1.80.0",
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "zcpequipmentapp",
    "type": "application",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "0.0.1"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:lrop",
      "version": "1.20.2",
      "toolsId": "4c2d36ce-a572-4ef5-a4cb-4bd72a531d54"
    },
    "crossNavigation": {
      "inbounds": {
        "Equipment-manage": {
          "semanticObject": "Equipment",
          "action": "manage",
          "title": "{{appTitle}}",
          "subTitle": "{{appDescription}}",
          "icon": "sap-icon://machine",
          "signature": {
            "parameters": {},
            "additionalParameters": "allowed"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "dataSources": {
      "ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN',Version='0001')/$value/",
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN.xml"
        }
      },
      "annotation": {
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
        }
      },
      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCP_SB_EQUIP/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": ["ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN", "annotation"],
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
          "odataVersion": "2.0"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "sap-icon://machine",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": true,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.142.6",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {},
        "sap.ushell": {},
        "sap.f": {},
        "sap.ui.comp": {},
        "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
        "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "zcpequipmentapp.i18n.i18n"
        }
      },
      "": {
        "dataSource": "mainService",
        "preload": true,
        "settings": {
          "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
          "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
          "refreshAfterChange": false,
          "metadataUrlParams": {
            "sap-value-list": "none"
          }
        }
      },
      "@i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
      }
    },
    "services": {
      "ShellUIService": {
        "factoryName": "sap.ushell.ui5service.ShellUIService"
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": []
    },
    "routing": {
      "config": {},
      "routes": [],
      "targets": {}
    }
  },
  "sap.ui.generic.app": {
    "_version": "1.3.0",
    "settings": {
      "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
      "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
      "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
      "showDraftToggle": false
    },
    "pages": {
      "ListReport|Equipment": {
        "entitySet": "Equipment",
        "component": {
          "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport",
          "list": true,
          "settings": {
            "condensedTableLayout": true,
            "smartVariantManagement": true,
            "enableTableFilterInPageVariant": true,
            "filterSettings": {
              "dateSettings": {
                "useDateRange": true
              }
            },
            "tableSettings": {
              "type": "ResponsiveTable"
            }
          }
        },
        "pages": {
          "ObjectPage|Equipment": {
            "entitySet": "Equipment",
            "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
            "component": {
              "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.fiori": {
    "registrationIds": ["F1234"],
    "archeType": "transactional"
  },
  "sap.cloud": {
    "public": true,
    "service": "zcpequipmentapp"
  }
}


mta.yaml

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: zcpequipmentapp
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    requires:
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
        parameters:
          content-target: true
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host-key
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa-key
    parameters:
      content:
        instance:
          destinations:
            - Name: zcpequipmentapp_html_repo_host
              ServiceInstanceName: zcpequipmentapp-html5-service
              ServiceKeyName: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host-key
              sap.cloud.service: zcpequipmentapp
            - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
              Name: zcpequipmentapp_uaa
              ServiceInstanceName: zcpequipmentapp-xsuaa-service
              ServiceKeyName: zcpequipmentapp-uaa-key
              sap.cloud.service: zcpequipmentapp
          existing_destinations_policy: update
    build-parameters:
      no-source: true

  - name: zcpequipmentapp-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - zcpequipmentapp.zip
          name: zcpequipmentapp
          target-path: resources/

  - name: zcpequipmentapp
    type: html5
    path: .
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-xsuaa-service
      service-plan: application
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-html5-service
      service-plan: app-host

parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true


destination

entry.title: KE30.
entry.content:
Hello! I need help, please.

KE30 does not pick up the direct posting from FI/MM. The cost classes are assigned to cost groups that have an associated value field.

Thanks

entry.title: How to set a GroupHeaderListItem inactive in tabular suggestion
entry.content:
Hello community. I am struggling with the grouping function for the tabular suggestion of an Input. The basic grouping works just fine but the created row (GroupHeaderListItem) is always active and selectable which confuses users. I have tryed the 

"groupHeaderFactory" function that is called correctly but the table itself seems to ignore the "type" property of the GroupHeaderListItem. See the code below:
  private createGroupHeader(groupTitle: { key: string }): GroupHeaderListItem {
    return new GroupHeaderListItem({ title: groupTitle.key, type: 'Inactive' });
  }

I am on version 1.139.0.

Is this a bug or is there an other way to get the GroupHeader inactive? Thanks.

/BR Tobias

entry.title: Fixed Price Condition in Subcontracting PO – Prevent Proration at GR
entry.content:
We have subcontracting POs where we agree with the vendor on a fixed manufacturing price. For example:

  • Agreed price to manufacture 100 units = $100

  • If at Goods Receipt (GR) the vendor delivers 99 units (due to yield/variance), the system should still keep the total agreed value of $100.

  • The system should NOT prorate the value based on the received quantity.

These POs normally:

  • Have only one line item

  • Do not allow partial deliveries

  • Are received only once

However, because this is a yield-based production scenario, sending the same quantity of components may result in 99, 100, or 101 finished units.

Additionally:

  • The component materials sent to the vendor have average price.

  • In many cases, the invoice is posted long after the GR.

  • The batch is often consumed before the actual invoice value is posted, which creates valuation inconsistencies if the GR value is prorated.

What I Tried

To fulfill the requirement, I created a new pricing condition as a copy of PBXX and adjusted some configuration settings.

The intention was to:

  • Apply a fixed total amount per PO item

  • Avoid any quantity-based proration at Goods Receipt

However, despite the changes, the system still prorates the condition value based on the received quantity.

Question

Has anyone successfully configured a pricing condition in a subcontracting PO that:

  • Applies a fixed total amount at item level

  • Does not prorate at GR based on actual received quantity

  • Keeps the full agreed amount even if GR quantity differs from PO quantity

If so:

Any guidance on the correct configuration approach would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Creation and maintenance of multiple app variants of the same app in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear community,

Customer is required adaptation and enhancement of standard SAP Fiori App for multiple countries/company codes/locations. As the result more than 20 different variants might be needed for the same app. In addition, target users are not capable to use Adapt UI properly as key-users so all changes are done centrally by development team. They are defining set of standard and custom fields that might be used for all locations and then limit them for each case.

While it is possible to create each variant (but quite difficult). it seems that maintaining it might be even harder - adding new field would require adaptation of all existing variants.

What is the best approach to manage this amount of variants?

What are the possible options to have dynamically defined variant configuration? 

What is the authorisation process to make sure that each location accesses only their app variant? (We need to block access to standard app and other variants)

 

Thank you,

BR Semyon

entry.title: Delta load from BWIP to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
We are working with four systems in sequence:
1. SAP S/4HANA
2. SAP CPM
3. SAP BW-IP
4. SAP Datasphere
The CPM data is managed in BW-IP, using standard content. Both CPM and BW-IP are embedded within S/4HANA.
Our key question is: How can we load data into SAP Datasphere using a replication flow with delta loads, rather than only initial loads?
We have already created a custom composite provider based on an Infocube (standard content), and a corresponding HANA view is available from the same composite provider. We attempted several approaches—including CDS views via HANA views, AMDP, table functions, and CDS views—but all failed.
We would like guidance on the correct way to enable delta loads into SAP Datasphere, whether the source is a composite provider or a HANA view.

entry.title: Standard Application Enhancement
entry.content: Are there other ways to enhance standard applications without changing its namespace and id aside from Key User Adaptation? I need to insert my custom code but as a requirement, the personalized views must remain so a new namespace and id for the app is not an option.

entry.title: Payment Terms Tab Grayed Out In Business Partner Data
entry.content:
In test environment, I've recently added a new payment term and assigned it to one of our business partners. I didn't realize that I changed some setting that made the payment terms tab grayed out. 

This seems to be a user profile issue since other user can access payment terms. 
I check my authorization and i have full access to Business Partner Master Data including Payment terms under it. 

I checked my licenses and i got the same from the users who can accessed it. 

I checked our live environment and i can access it. 

It's only in test environment and my profile that has payment terms grayed out

How to access the payment terms tab?

entry.title: How to mass create supplier organizations for a sourcing event in ARIBA
entry.content: We usually create supplier organization by submitting supplier creation requests through SLP questionnaire form. However, we need to invite 90+ suppliers to a sourcing event, so submitting the SLP supplier creation request form for each supplier is not an option. Is there a way to mass create supplier organization for Guided Sourcing events?

entry.title: User default values from Fiori Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

How we can retrieve user's default values from fiori launchpad in a custom UI5 application?
I checked in sap ushell services but could not find any option.
Please suggest.


Thank you!

entry.title: Learning hub
entry.content:
Hi,

I ve ordered a lerning hub subscription on Thursday but haven’t gotten any login. Is this normal? Should I just be patient or did anything go wrong? 

Thanks for your time.

entry.title: Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigates to transient $uid path instead of server draft path,
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing an issue in a CAP + Fiori Elements OData V4 draft application

Problem:

 When I press Create in one specific config app (AggrConfigService / Aggregator), the draft creation flow breaks.

The UI navigates/binds to a transient client-side path like: AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)

CAP then returns: 404 Invalid resource path "AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=...)" What is strange is that the POST itself succeeds first.

Observed request/response

1) Create request: POST Aggregator Body: {"AggregatorID":"Test"}

Response: HTTP/1.1 204 No Content location: Aggregator.drafts('Test') 2) Immediately after that, UI5 sends: GET Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)?$select=AggregatorID,AggregatorName,HasActiveEntity,HasDraftEntity,IsActive,IsActiveEntity&$expand=DraftAdministrativeData($select=DraftIsCreatedByMe,DraftUUID,InProcessByUser) Response: HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found {"error":{"code":"404","message":"Invalid resource path \"AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)\""}}

So the backend seems to create the draft successfully and returns a draft location, but the frontend still tries to read via the transient $uid path instead of switching to the canonical draft path.

Environment:

SAP CAP Node.js - @sap/cds 8.9.8 - SAPUI5 1.142.0 - Fiori Elements OData V4 - Draft-enabled service - Cloud Foundry deployment on BTP

Questions:

1) Has anyone seen Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigate to a transient `$uid` path like this after a successful POST?

2) Is there any known issue/pattern where FE object-page routing or binding keeps the transient context instead of resolving to the canonical draft location returned by CAP?

Thanks a lot.

Best Regards,

Muthu

SAPUI5 SAP Fiori Elements SAP Cloud Application Programming Model  OData SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: How to have multiple schedules in service call whenever there is a contract?
entry.content:
Hi,
I'm unable to add another schedule when the service call has a contract linked to it.
The service call is still open, and I can enter the schedule details in the row, but it won’t save.
I tested another service call without a contract, and in that case I can add additional schedules without any issue.
The contract is active (not terminated), and in the Document Settings for Service Calls, the option “Scheduling of multiple tickets” is enabled. I also have full authorization for the Service Call module.

Can anyone help me? 
Thanks

entry.title: Running Hugging Face AI Models with SAPUI5 and CAPM
entry.content:
Hi everyone 👋

After spending several hours researching and experimenting, I finally found a simple way to answer a question that many SAP developers have started asking:

“Is it possible to run AI models inside SAP applications using SAPUI5 and CAPM?”

The short answer is:

   Yes, absolutely!

In this blog, I will share how we can integrate Hugging Face AI models with SAPUI5 and CAPM, and how we can build a simple AI-powered application that can generate text responses and even images.

 

                                                                        Fig 1.1 Hugging Face Models sections

Now the Interesting Part: Can SAPUI5 Use AI?

        When I started this experiment, my first question was:

Can SAPUI5 directly call an AI model?

        The answer is yes, especially for text generation models.

To make this work, we only need three things:

- A Hugging Face account
-  An HF API Token
-  A model endpoint

Once you log in to Hugging Face, you can generate an HF Token from your account settings.

Then you can choose any model depending on your use case. Hugging Face provides model benchmarks and descriptions, so you can easily decide which one is best for:

  • Education
  • Coding
  • Reasoning
  • Chat
  • Content generation

Connecting Hugging Face with SAPUI5

Now let's talk about the implementation.

In the SAPUI5 controller, we can send a request to the Hugging Face API using JavaScript fetch().

The UI simply takes a user prompt, sends it to the AI model, and displays the response.

Example flow:

User enters a prompt:

       What is Hugging Face and SAPUI5?

SAPUI5 sends the prompt to the Hugging Face model.

The model generates a response and returns it.

Then SAPUI5 displays the result directly on the UI.

This makes it very easy to build AI chat assistants inside SAP applications.

 

                                                                                    Fig 1.2 Output- Hugging Face Ai Chat

 

Sample Code:

              Fig 1.3 sapui5 Controller.js - Hugging Face Ai Chat

 

                      Fig 1.3 sapui5 view.xml - Hugging Face Ai Chat

But What About Image Generation?

      This is where things become interesting.

      When I tried running image generation models directly in SAPUI5, I realized something important.

      SAPUI5 works best with text-based AI APIs.

      For image generation models, we need a backend service.

This is where CAPM (Cloud Application Programming Model) becomes very useful.

 

Using CAPM for Image Generation

To generate images, we can integrate fal.ai, which provides inference APIs for advanced image generation models.

Some examples include:

  • black-forest-labs / FLUX.1-dev
  • Tongyi-MAI / Z-Image-Turbo

These models can generate high-quality AI images from text prompts.

For example:

"Portrait of a futuristic astronaut with glowing blue eyes"

The system sends this prompt to the AI model, and the model generates an image.

 

How CAPM Handles This

Inside the CAPM Node.js service, we create a custom action such as:

     generateImage

This action receives the prompt from SAPUI5.

Then CAPM calls the fal.ai API with that prompt.

The AI model generates the image and returns a URL of the generated image, which we send back to the UI.

This makes CAPM act as a bridge between SAPUI5 and AI models.

 

Why Use fal.ai Instead of Hugging Face Directly?

You might ask:

If we already use Hugging Face, why do we need fal.ai?

   Good question.

Some models listed on Hugging Face are actually hosted through different inference providers, including fal.ai.

For example:

"black-forest-labs / FLUX.1-dev"

This model is often executed through fal.ai inference APIs.

So fal.ai becomes an easy way to run those models with strong GPU performance.

Sample Code:

                   Fig 1.4 Output – CAPM - Hugging face Image generation

 

 

                  Fig 1.5 service.js - first half- Hugging face Image generation

 

                  Fig 1.6 service.js - second half - Hugging face Image generation

 

               Fig 1.7 Fal.ai Api key Generation - Hugging face Image generation

 

Final Result

After connecting everything together, we get a simple AI application:

The application can:

✅Generate AI text responses using SAPUI5
✅Generate AI images using CAPM services
✅Use Hugging Face models
✅Run without training your own model

All of this can be done using free developer APIs, unless you need large-scale GPU workloads.

 

Conclusion

From this experiment, we can see that SAP applications can easily integrate with modern AI platforms.

The architecture becomes very simple:

                                         SAPUI5
                                              ⬇
                                  CAPM Service
                                             ⬇
                    Hugging Face / fal.ai AI Models

 

Using this approach, developers can build powerful applications such as:

  • AI chat assistants inside SAP
  • AI-powered knowledge systems
  • Image generation tools
  • Smart enterprise automation

And the best part is that you can even deploy your own AI model on Hugging Face and use it inside SAP applications.

 

⭐I hope this small experiment helps other developers who are curious about AI + SAP integration.

Happy coding! 🚀

entry.title: Implementing a Public/Anonymous SAPUI5 Registration App on BTP with Backend Data Validation
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

I am working on a registration application using SAPUI5 on SAP BTP. I have a prototype ready, but I need guidance on two specific technical issues:

  1. Deployment for public access: How can I deploy this registration app so it is publicly available for anyone to use without requiring a login or IAS authentication? I need the app to be accessible to external users who do not have system credentials.
  2. Accessing system data for verification: How can I securely access our backend system data from this public application? The goal is to verify if a customer already exists in our system (e.g., checking by Email or BP) before they register. Since the user is anonymous, what is the best way to handle the connection to the backend?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Possible extension for fiori app "Stage for Production"
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I've been asked to add into the Fiori app "Stage for production", two fields that are already available into PMR, at item level. The fields are "Operation Number or Activity Number" (/SCWM/S_SP_A_ITEM_PRDP - /SCWM/OPERATION) and "Operation Short Description" (/SCWM/S_SP_A_ITEM_PRDP - /SCWM/OPERATION_DESCR).

Do you know if it's somehow possible to extend the app to add these two fields?

Is it somehow possible via SPRO?

Thank you for your support

entry.title: Question Regarding SAPUI5 3D Viewer Background
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the SAPUI5 3D Viewer.

Is it possible to change the background of the scene to a 2D image and then load a 3D CAD file on top of that background?

If possible, could you let me know the method?

Thank you.

entry.title: Question about SAPUI5 3D Viewer
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the SAPUI5 3D Viewer.

Is it possible to change the scene background by loading a 2D image, and then load a 3D model on top of that background?

If so, could you please explain how to achieve this?

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,
Yonggyu Choi
